name: opus
description: Auto-route tasks to the cheapest Claude model that works correctly. Three-tier progression: Haiku → Sonnet → Opus. Classify before responding. HAIKU (default): factual Q&A, greetings, reminders, status checks, lookups, simple file ops, heartbeats, casual chat, 1-2 sentence tasks. ESCALATE TO SONNET: code >10 lines, analysis, comparisons, planning, reports, multi-step reasoning, tables, long writing >3 paragraphs, summarization, research synthesis, most user conversations. ESCALATE TO OPUS: architecture decisions, complex debugging, multi-file refactoring, strategic planning, nuanced judgment, deep research, critical production decisions. Rule: If a human needs >30 seconds of focused thinking, escalate. If Sonnet struggles with complexity, go to Opus. Save 50-90% on API costs by starting cheap and escalating only when needed.

Deployment friction arises from OAuth setup and credential management. Operational risk stems from email being mission-critical and subject to rate limits, auth revocation, or inbox parsing fragility—moderating the score from 5 to 4.
name: Email
description: Email management and automation. Send, read, search, and organize emails across multiple providers.

skillability_rationale: The skill automates a well-defined set of Goodreads interactions (search, detail retrieval, recommendations, list management) via browser automation, making it a coherent workflow-level capability. However, reliance on fragile browser automation — subject to UI changes, CAPTCHAs, and anti-bot measures — reduces robustness, interface clarity (selectors/flows may not be well-documented or stable), and composability (hard to chain with non-browser-based tools). Deployment friction is moderate due to browser environment requirements (e.g., headless Chrome, session persistence), and operational risk is elevated due to Goodreads' lack of official API support and potential terms-of-service violations. Still, it delivers tangible automation value for book-related tasks where no official API exists.
name: goodreads
description: Search for books, get book details and reviews, discover personalized recommendations, and manage reading lists on Goodreads — all through browser automation.
